caption.text: Alright, I'll give you 1 underrated UX shortcut,
but one that turns a frustrating moment into an engaging one.
The loading screen that adds value.
I wasn't too inspired for the name of this shortcut.
Most SaaS that have a loading screen
always offer the same thing:
a spinner, a progress bar,
and sometimes even nothing at all.
Except during this time your user is waiting,
they're staring into space,
they wonder if it's bugged, they might even refresh the page,
they open a new tab and sometimes they don't even come back.
Imagine an HR SaaS, the recruiter just imported 15 resumes at once.
The tool analyzes them, scores them,
ranks them, it's loading, 5 seconds, 10 seconds.
In the classic scenario there's often a spinner spinning,
the user waits, then gets impatient and doubts. Now
imagine the same scenario, but during the loading
they can see different things.
First, we tell them very clearly that it's loading.
Then we can offer them a product tip:
"you can filter your candidates by match score with one click
from the main dashboard"
It's simple, it's useful and immediate.
We can also very well
suggest discovering a feature they've never used,
with a preview image
and just below: "create customized evaluation grids
to score your candidates based on your own criteria"
Here, the user sees what the feature looks like,
they understand its value and without any interruption.
This 2nd scenario completely changes the game.
Because you turn a moment of passive waiting into a moment of discovery.
Your user
sees that it's coming and they learn something they didn't know how to do yet.
Then, because you reduce the perception of waiting time,
a loading screen with content always seems shorter than an empty one.
And finally, because you suggest feature discovery
without interrupting your user's journey.
No modal,
no intrusive tooltip,
but just at the right time, when they have nothing else to do.
Result, your user no longer suffers through the loading,
they discover your product while they wait
and they arrive at their dashboard already knowing what to do.
